name: Esquire Fortnightly's Eastern Establishment Chili
recipeIngredient: 5 lbs Beef brisket, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
6 Garlic cloves, large and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the kidney beans in a bowl and cover with water. Soak overnight.
2. Pour the beans and soaking water into a heavy saucepan. Bring to a boil over high heat. Lower the heat and simmer, covered, for about 1 hour or until tender. Stir occasionally.
3. Heat the oil or melt the lard in a large heavy casserole over medium heat. Pat the brisket dry and add it to the casserole. Cook the beef until brown on all sides. Remove and set aside.
4. Add more oil if needed, then add onions and garlic. Cook until onions are translucent. Stir in bell peppers, basil, bay leaf, ground red chile, cayenne pepper, Chile Caribe, and cumin. Cook for about 1 minute.
5. Return the brisket to the casserole. Add chopped tomatoes and their liquid. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer, uncovered, for 2 hours. Stir occasionally.
6. Stir in salt and black pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings. Add coffee and simmer, uncovered, for an additional hour.
7. Add kidney beans to the chili. Simmer, uncovered, for another half hour.
